title: Elyse Bouey
content:
People.ContactEmail ebouey@mccarthy.ca
- University of Calgary
Centred on client priorities, Elyse helps organizations advance major energy, power and infrastructure projects by navigating complex regulatory, environmental and Indigenous law matters with clarity and confidence.
Elyse Bouey is a Partner in McCarthy Tétrault's Business Law Group in the Calgary office. Her practice focuses on energy regulation, as well as environmental, administrative and Indigenous law issues, supporting clients involved in large‑scale energy, power and infrastructure projects across Canada and in cross‑border markets.
She works closely with clients to design sophisticated regulatory strategies, support meaningful Indigenous engagement, and secure the approvals required to move transformative projects forward.
She brings deep expertise in electricity transmission and distribution, conventional and renewable power generation, energy storage, upstream oil and gas, pipelines, petrochemicals, liquefied natural gas exports, hydrogen and ammonia projects, nuclear power, critical minerals and mining, and carbon capture, utilization and storage. Her work includes advising on project approvals, commercial transactions, tolls and tariffs, compliance proceedings, environmental and regulatory matters, Indigenous consultation, and the purchase and sale of carbon credits and other environmental attributes.
She has appeared before a variety of federal, provincial and territorial regulatory agencies, as well as all levels of court in Alberta, and has represented clients in several of the largest energy developments in Canada.
Active in the industry, she is a member of Women+Power, the Canadian Renewable Energy Association, the Independent Power Producers Society of Alberta, and Energy Storage Canada, where she participates in the Alberta Market Working Group. She has also volunteered as a member of the Board of Directors of the Central Alberta Community Legal Clinic.
She regularly contributes to industry dialogue through writing and speaking on emerging legal and regulatory issues.
She holds a JD from the University of Calgary’s Faculty of Law and a BA (Honours) from McGill University, and is called to the Alberta Bar. She has also completed additional training in utility capital budgeting and planning, gender‑based analysis plus (GBA+), and Indigenous cultural awareness through the Canadian Bar Association’s The Path program.
- Co-author, Powering Canada's Future: National Energy Corridor Partnership Announced, March 13, 2026.
- Co-author, From Policy to Practice: Canada's Evolving Environmental Law in 2025, November 24, 2025.
- Co-author, Implementation Framework for the Right to a Healthy Environment Under CEPA, November 11, 2025.
- Co-author, Recent Legislative and Regulatory Developments of Interest to Energy Lawyers, Alberta Law Review , Vol. 62, No. 2, January 8, 2025.
- Co-author, Now That the Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act Has Been Determined to Be Constitutionally Valid – What Does it All Mean?, March 29, 2021.
